hero.subtitle: A legendary cave where flamenco is not acted, it is lived. Feel the heartbeat of the zambra at the origin of Sacromonte.

legacy.title: The Legacy Continues
legacy.subtitle: Daughters of the founder, principal dancers, and guardians of authenticity.
legacy.p1: Founded by the legendary Juanillo Heredia, Cueva Venta El Gallo is more than a tablao; it is a home. Today, his three daughters are the direct heirs of that unbreakable lineage.
legacy.p2: They not only manage this origin, but they also deploy their power on stage every night, ensuring that the strength and technique maintain the prestigious standard set by their father.

narrative.p1: Since 1996, our whitewashed walls have vibrated with the pure truth of flamenco. An intimate stage where the soleá and the seguiriya reach you just inches away, fusing the spectator with the artist.
narrative.feature_1_desc: The echo reverberates with perfect fidelity, amplifying the footwork without any distortion.
narrative.feature_2_desc: There are no barriers. The heat of the dance and the strength of the singing are felt directly in the soul.

faq.subtitle: We answer your questions so you only have to worry about feeling the Sacromonte.
faq.q1: What time does the Zambra Gitana show start?
faq.a1: We offer two daily shows to guarantee an intimate experience. The first show starts at 19:00 hrs and the second at 21:00 hrs. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early to get settled and enjoy the atmosphere of the cave.
faq.q2: Do I need to book dinner along with the show?
faq.a2: It is not strictly necessary, but highly recommended. Our gastronomy is part of the Sacromonte heritage and perfectly complements the evening. We have a Tradition Menu and a Premium Tapas menu that can be enjoyed before or during the show.
faq.q3: How do I get to Cueva Venta El Gallo?
faq.a3: We are located in the heart of Sacromonte, facing the impressive viewpoint of the Alhambra. You can arrive by taxi right to the door (Barranco de los Negros, 5), take the C34 minibus from Plaza Nueva, or walk up through the historic Albaicín neighborhood.
faq.q4: Is it suitable for small children?
faq.a4: Flamenco is universal passion. Children are welcome to feel the strength of our art. However, out of respect for the purity of the cave's acoustic sound, we recommend the first show for families.
faq.q5: Do the artists change every night?
faq.a5: Our family is the core. Juanillo Heredia's daughters are the soul of Venta El Gallo, performing almost every night. We also have top-tier dancers, singers, and guitarists who rotate to ensure each zambra is a live and unique show.

reviews.subtitle: It is not us who certify the quality of our Zambra. It is the heartbeats of those who have experienced it from within.
reviews.social_subtitle: We document the daily magic and life in the cave. Join the Sacromonte community.

r1.text: The most authentic experience we had in Granada. Being a meter away from the stage while they dance and sing gives you goosebumps. The tasting menu was impeccable.

r2.text: Pure raw emotion. The acoustics of the cave combined with the passionate performance left us speechless. The daughters of Juanillo truly honor his legacy. A must-do.

r3.text: Magical. No microphones, just the cajón, the guitar, the singing, and the heels. You breathe art and professionalism. You can tell that for them the stage is not work, it is their life.

r4.text: I've been to several tablaos in Andalusia and none has transmitted the same intimacy as Venta El Gallo. The cave is spectacular and the staff treatment is exquisite.

reels.subtitle: Real atmosphere, no filters. This is how a night at Venta El Gallo is experienced.

history_page.title: The Legacy
history_page.subtitle: A century carving the rock so that flamenco resonates in its purest form. Meet the guardians of Sacromonte.
history_page.cta_subtitle: The cave awaits you. Come and feel the weight of the legacy live with our gastronomy and the original Zambra Gitana.

e1.title: The Origin in the Rock
e1.desc: Juanillo Heredia acquires the cave in the mythical ravine of Los Negros. What began by excavating the rock would soon become a sanctuary of pure singing and dancing, cementing our legend.

e2.title: The Origin of the Zambra
e2.desc: We define our format of 'Zambra Gitana'. Word of mouth, sweat, and passion attract personalities from all over the world. The natural acoustics of lime and rock are revealed as our greatest treasure.

e3.title: Prestigious Award
e3.desc: Venta El Gallo receives the Tourism Prestige Award of Granada. An official recognition that validates decades of uninterrupted dedication to the preservation of flamenco art in its most visceral state.

e4.title: The Blood Continues
e4.desc: Today, the direct heiresses watch over the fire. They not only guard the origin, but also impose their respect on stage every night, merging uncompromising tradition with the highest gastronomy.

hero.desc: Meet the masters who bring the Gypsy Zambra to life every night. A lineage of pure art forged in the depths of Sacromonte.

cta.desc_3: . Don't miss the opportunity to experience true flamenco art from the front row.

artists_page.footer: "Flamenco at Venta El Gallo is not rehearsed, it is inherited."

artists.name: Jara Heredia
artists.description: Jara Heredia began her artistic career with teachers such as Angustillas Ruiz "la mona" Manolete, Eva Garrido "la Yerbabuena", Antonio Canales, Farruquito, Manuela Carrasco... forming part of Mario Maya's scenic school. She has performed on several occasions for H.M. King Juan Carlos and in Los Angeles at Antonio Banderas' house, at the International Festival of Music and Dance of Granada, Lorca Poetry Prize Festival in New York 2004, USA Festival 2010, Carthage International Festival.

artists.name: Antonia Heredia
artists.description: Antonia Heredia began her artistic career with dancer Angustias Ruiz and her sister Jara Heredia. She later began to train professionally with teachers such as Mario Maya, Eduardo Serrano «guiri», Belén Maya, Rafaela Carrasco, Manuel Liñán... She started performing in the flamenco tablaos of Granada at the age of 12. She performed at the music and dance festivals of Granada. She has performed with artists such as Juan Moneo «El tortas» at the Almuñécar festival, Pepe Habichuela and Josemi Carmona on a tour throughout India and the Mont de Marsan Festival with Pepe Luis Carmona in numerous festivals. She participated with Antonio Canales in the corral del carbón in several music videos of the stature of «Manzanita, Remedios Amaya, Antonio Carmona, Pepe Luis Carmona...»

artists.name: Dinastía Heredia
artists.description: The feminine soul of Venta El Gallo. Juan Heredia's daughters represent the historical continuity and unwavering commitment to pure art that their father initiated.

artists.name: Antonio Heredia «El Chonico»
artists.description: An absolute benchmark of the Granada touch. His guitar is the untamed heartbeat of the cave, combining refined technique with the ancestral echo of the Sacromonte neighborhood.

artists.name: Miguel Ángel Cortés
artists.description: Internationally recognized virtuosity and sensitivity. Awarded the Bordón Minero, he is one of the most influential guitarists of his generation in the flamenco scene.

artists.name: Paco Fernández
artists.description: Strength and charisma on the tablao. Following in the footsteps of great masters, Paco prints a visceral energy that makes the foundations of the Zambra vibrate in every performance.

artists.name: Coral Fernández
artists.description: Elegance and refined technique. Trained in the best schools of flamencology, her dance is an ode to Granada aesthetics, full of temperament and absolute precision.

artists.name: Raimundo Benítez
artists.description: Master of movement. His trajectory in international companies allows him to bring an interpretive depth that elevates dance to new heights of expression.

artists.name: Antonio Heredia
artists.description:
Antonio Heredia is a Granada singer with deep flamenco roots, born into a family of artists where singing was always a way of life. From a young age he showed an innate talent, soaking up traditional songs and developing a powerful and expressive voice. At 19 he began his professional career in the tablaos of Granada, standing out for his versatility and ability to move the public.

He has worked with great figures of flamenco, such as Los Farruco and Belén López, and has participated in festivals both in Spain and internationally. In 2021 he released the album Hasta La Eternidad, available on digital platforms, and has been part of shows alongside artists such as Sergio Gómez "El Colorao" and Alicia Morales. His singing, full of feeling and authenticity, fuses tradition with a personal style that has consolidated him as one of the most respected voices in current flamenco.

hero.desc: Looking for a magical place for your next event? In our Sacromonte cave, where flamenco is born and history is breathed in every corner, we create unique moments that are etched forever.

services.title_1: Do you want to celebrate a special event with
services.desc: Special celebrations, company dinners, trips with friends or cultural meetings... Whatever the occasion, we turn it into an unforgettable experience. We combine the best of Andalusian gastronomy with the most authentic flamenco art, in an incomparable setting with views of the Alhambra and the charm of Granada.
